name: trimesh (Python 3D Mesh Processing)

performance.notes: NumPy-backed; fast for mesh queries; ray casting via rtree; boolean ops via manifold3d

agent_readiness.notes: Clean Python 3D mesh library. import trimesh. mesh = trimesh.load('model.stl'). mesh.volume. mesh.is_watertight. mesh.bounds. mesh.sample(1000) — surface point sampling. mesh.section(plane_origin, plane_normal). Boolean ops: trimesh.boolean.union(a, b). Convex hull: mesh.convex_hull. Ray casting: mesh.ray.intersects_location(origins, dirs). Collision manager. Export: mesh.export('out.obj'). Formats: STL, OBJ, PLY, GLB, GLTF, 3MF. Voxelization. Scene graph. pyrender integration. Used in robotics, 3D printing, and game dev.

gotchas: Boolean operations require manifold3d or blender backend (not built-in)
Non-manifold meshes cause issues with volume/watertight checks
GPU rendering requires pyrender or open3d separately
Some format loaders require optional dependencies (assimp, rtree)
best_when: 3D mesh loading, analysis, and manipulation in Python
STL/OBJ/GLTF file processing for 3D printing or robotics
Ray casting and collision detection in Python
avoid_when: Large point clouds without mesh (use open3d or numpy directly)
summary: trimesh is a clean Python library for 3D mesh processing. trimesh.load('model.stl') loads STL/OBJ/PLY/GLTF. mesh.volume, mesh.is_watertight, mesh.bounds. mesh.sample(1000) for surface point sampling. Ray casting: mesh.ray.intersects_location(). Boolean ops (requires manifold3d). Voxelization. Export to 20+ formats. Used in robotics (collision checking), 3D printing (validation), and game dev. MIT license. Simpler API than Open3D for pure mesh operations.
